2606.31742.title: FedXDS: Leveraging Model Attribution Methods to counteract Data Heterogeneity in Federated Learning
2606.31742.paper_summary: Explainable AI (XAI) methods have demonstrated significant success in recent years at identifying relevant features in input data that drive deep learning model decisions, enhancing interpretability for users. However, the potential of XAI beyond providing model transparency has remained largely unexplored in adjacent machine learning domains. In this paper, we show for the first time how XAI can be utilized in the context of federated learning. Specifically, while federated learning enables collaborative model training without raw data sharing, it suffers from performance degradation when client data distributions exhibit statistical heterogeneity. We introduce FedXDS (Federated Learning via XAI-guided Data Sharing), the first approach to utilize feature attribution techniques to identify precisely which data elements should be selectively shared between clients to mitigate heterogeneity. By employing propagation-based attribution, our method identifies task-relevant features through a single backward pass, enabling selective data sharing that aligns client contributions. To protect sensitive information, we incorporate metric privacy techniques that provide formal privacy guarantees while preserving utility. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach consistently achieves higher accuracy and faster convergence compared to existing methods across varying client numbers and heterogeneity settings. We provide theoretical privacy guarantees and empirically demonstrate robustness against both membership inference and feature inversion attacks. Code is available at https://github.com/MaxH1996/FedXDS.
2606.31742.paper_summary_zh: 可解釋人工智慧（XAI）方法在最近幾年中已顯示出顯著的成功，能夠識別驅動深度學習模型決策的輸入數據中的相關特徵，增強了用戶的可解釋性。<lb/>然而，XAI在提供模型透明度之外的潛力在相鄰的機器學習領域中仍然大多未被探索。<lb/>在本文中，我們首次展示了如何在聯邦學習的背景下利用XAI。<lb/>具體而言，雖然聯邦學習使得在不共享原始數據的情況下進行協作模型訓練成為可能，但當客戶端數據分佈顯示統計異質性時，它會遭受性能下降。<lb/>我們介紹了FedXDS（通過XAI引導的數據共享的聯邦學習），這是第一種利用特徵歸因技術來精確識別應該在客戶端之間選擇性共享的數據元素，以減輕異質性的方式。<lb/>通過使用基於傳播的歸因，我們的方法通過單次反向傳播識別任務相關特徵，實現與客戶端貢獻對齊的選擇性數據共享。<lb/>為了保護敏感信息，我們納入了度量隱私技術，這些技術在保持效用的同時提供正式的隱私保證。<lb/>實驗結果表明，我們的方法在不同的客戶端數量和異質性設置下，始終實現了比現有方法更高的準確性和更快的收斂速度。<lb/>我們提供了理論上的隱私保證，並實證展示了對成員推斷和特徵反轉攻擊的穩健性。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/MaxH1996/FedXDS 獲得。
2606.31742.author: Maximilian Andreas Hoefler et.al.
2606.31742.authors: Maximilian Andreas Hoefler, Karsten Mueller, Wojciech Samek
